text: The roots of education are bitter, but the fruit is sweet.
author: Aristotle

text: Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: The beautiful thing about learning is that no one can take it away from you.
author: B.B. King

text: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Study hard, for the well is deep, and our brains are shallow.
author: Richard Baxter

text: The expert in anything was once a beginner.
author: Helen Hayes

text: There is no elevator to success. You have to take the stairs.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: The more that you read, the more things you will know. The more that you learn, the more places you'll go.
author: Dr. Seuss

text: Don't let what you cannot do interfere with what you can do.
author: John Wooden

text: There are no shortcuts to any place worth going.
author: Beverly Sills

text: Learning never exhausts the mind.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: You don't understand anything until you learn it more than one way.
author: Marvin Minsky

text: You can never be overdressed or overeducated.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: In a world where you can be anything, be kind and curious.
author: Jennifer Dukes Lee

text: To acquire knowledge, one must study; but to acquire wisdom, one must observe.
author: Marilyn vos Savant

text: Knowledge is power. Information is liberating. Education is the premise of progress, in every society, in every family.
author: Kofi Annan

text: Success is no accident. It is hard work, perseverance, learning, studying, sacrifice and most of all, love of what you are doing.
author: Pelé

text: Procrastination is the thief of time.
author: Edward Young

text: Focus on your goals, not your fear.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: Education is not the learning of facts, but the training of the mind to think.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Research is what I'm doing when I don't know what I'm doing.
author: Wernher von Braun

text: Genius is 1% talent and 99% hard work.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Never discourage anyone who continually makes progress, no matter how slow.
author: Plato

text: The only person who is educated is the one who has learned how to learn and change.
author: Carl Rogers

text: Develop a passion for learning. If you do, you will never cease to grow.
author: Anthony J. D'Angelo

text: Tell me and I forget, teach me and I may remember, involve me and I learn.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Nothing will work unless you do.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Education is our passport to the future, for tomorrow belongs to the people who prepare for it today.
author: Malcolm X

text: It is not that I'm so smart. But I stay with the questions much longer.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Self-education is, I firmly believe, the only kind of education there is.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: The mind is not a vessel to be filled, but a fire to be kindled.
author: Plutarch

text: Wisdom is not a product of schooling but of the lifelong attempt to acquire it.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Who questions much, shall learn much, and retain much.
author: Francis Bacon

text: You can teach a student a lesson for a day; but if you can teach him to learn by creating curiosity, he will continue the learning process as long as he lives.
author: Clay P. Bedford

text: Recipe for success: Study while others are sleeping; work while others are loafing; prepare while others are playing; and dream while others are wishing.
author: William A. Ward

text: Knowledge will bring you the opportunity to make a difference.
author: Claire Fagin

text: A room without books is like a body without a soul.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: Education breeds confidence. Confidence breeds hope. Hope breeds peace.
author: Confucius

text: The purpose of learning is growth, and our minds, unlike our bodies, can continue growing as we continue to live.
author: Mortimer Adler
